bins.name: Blue Recycling
bins.description: Clean, dry recyclables: paper, cardboard, plastic bottles/containers (#1-#7), glass, aluminum/steel cans

bins.name: Green Organics
bins.description: Food scraps, yard waste, food-soiled paper (SB 1383 compliant)

bins.name: Black Landfill
bins.description: Non-recyclable, non-compostable waste that must go to landfill

items.name: Greasy Pizza Box
items.explanation: Food-soiled cardboard goes in the green organics cart, not recycling. Clean cardboard goes in blue.

items.name: Empty Plastic Water Bottle
items.explanation: PET (#1) plastic bottles are widely recyclable. Rinse and remove cap before recycling.

items.name: Banana Peel
items.explanation: Food scraps go in the green organics cart per SB 1383 requirements.

items.name: Styrofoam Takeout Container
items.explanation: Expanded polystyrene (Styrofoam) is not recyclable in Cerritos curbside collection and goes to landfill.

items.name: Aluminum Soda Can
items.explanation: Aluminum is infinitely recyclable and highly valuable. Rinse before placing in blue cart.

items.name: Used Coffee Grounds & Filter
items.explanation: Coffee grounds and paper filters are organic material — perfect for the green cart.

items.name: Plastic Grocery Bag
items.explanation: Plastic bags tangle recycling machinery and should NOT go in the blue cart. Return to grocery store drop-offs or place in black cart.

items.name: Stack of Newspapers
items.explanation: Clean paper and newspaper are recyclable. Keep dry and place in blue cart.

items.name: Grass Clippings & Leaves
items.explanation: Yard waste goes in the green organics cart for composting.

items.name: Shiny Chip Bag (Mylar)
items.explanation: Multi-layer flexible packaging (Mylar/foil-lined bags) is not recyclable curbside and goes to landfill.

items.name: Glass Pasta Sauce Jar
items.explanation: Glass jars are recyclable. Rinse out food residue and remove the metal lid (also recyclable).

items.name: Used Paper Napkin
items.explanation: Food-soiled paper products go in the green organics cart. Clean paper towels/napkins also accepted.

items.name: Cardboard Egg Carton
items.explanation: Clean cardboard egg cartons are recyclable in the blue cart. Foam egg cartons go in black.

items.name: Used Diaper
items.explanation: Diapers are landfill-only waste. They contain mixed materials that cannot be separated for recycling.

items.name: Paper Milk Carton
items.explanation: Carton-board containers (milk, juice, broth) are recyclable. Rinse and flatten.

items.name: Meat Packaging Tray (with absorbent pad)
items.explanation: Meat trays with absorbent pads are contaminated and go to landfill. The foam tray alone is also not recyclable in most curbside programs.

sb1383.title: SB 1383 — California's Organic Waste Law
sb1383.description: Senate Bill 1383 (2016, effective Jan 2022) requires California to reduce organic waste in landfills by 75% by 2025. All residents must separate food scraps and yard waste into the green organics cart. This reduces methane emissions from landfills, which is a potent greenhouse gas.
keyFacts: Organic waste in landfills produces methane — 80x more potent than CO2 over 20 years
California sends ~23 million tons of organic waste to landfills annually
SB 1383 target: 75% reduction in organic waste disposal by 2025
All Cerritos residents must use the green cart for food scraps and yard waste
Athens Services operates all Cerritos waste collection using CNG-powered vehicles
